page.title: Chief Cleric of the Imperial Household

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:掌典職]]
{{Cite|Shōten-shoku (掌典職)|qq=}} The '''Shōten-shoku''' (掌典職) is the division responsible for conducting {{ill|Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|en|Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|qid=Q11453226|lt=Ceremonies of the Japanese Imperial Palace|1=Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace}} rituals within the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|qid=Q909452|lt=Imperial Household of Japan|1=Imperial House of Japan}}. These duties are carried out primarily within the {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|en|Three Palace Sanctuaries|qid=Q7797685|lt=Three Palace Sanctuaries|1=Three Palace Sanctuaries}}. The current Shōtenchō (掌典長, Chief Ritualist) is {{ill|Masato Kaji|ja|加地正人_(警察官僚)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masato Kaji|1=Masato Kaji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q119687938}}, a former police bureaucrat and former Deputy Grand Chamberlain. Before 1947, the Shōten-shoku was part of the {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household|en|Ministry of the Imperial Household|qid=Q8193428|lt=Imperial Household Ministry|1=Ministry of the Imperial Household}}, functioning as a state organ. == Shintō Ministry Era == In {{ill|1871|en|1871|qid=Q7746|lt=Meiji 4|1=1871}} (1871), roles related to Imperial Shinto rituals at the Kyūchū Sanden were established within the {{ill|Ministry of Divinities|en|Ministry of Divinities|qid=Q4294696|lt=Ministry of Divinities|1=Ministry of Divinities}} as the offices of Daishōten, Chūshōten, and Shōshōten.{{Sfn|『神道事典』|p=159|loc=嶋津宣史「掌典」|qq=}} == Shikibu Bureau Era == Responsibility for these rituals was transferred to the {{ill|Shikibu Bureau|ja|式部寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu Bureau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.{{Sfn|『神道事典』|p=159|loc=嶋津宣史「掌典」|qq=}} == Imperial Household Ministry's Shōten Division == In 1907, the "Imperial Household Ministry Ordinance" (Meiji 40th Year {{ill|Imperial Household Ordinance|ja|皇室令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Ordinance|1=Imperial Household Ordinance|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11581032}} No. 3) was enacted, outlining the Shōten-shoku's functions and hierarchy within the {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household|en|Ministry of the Imperial Household|qid=Q8193428|lt=Imperial Household Ministry|1=Ministry of the Imperial Household}}.{{Sfn|『官報』第7304号(明治)|pp=1–4|qq=}} The roles and regulations included: * '''Shōtenchō''': Supervises the division and its officials, overseeing the performance of Imperial Household rituals. * '''Shōten Jichō''': Deputy, assisting the Shōtenchō and acting as their proxy when necessary. * '''Shōten''': Eight individuals responsible for specific ritual tasks. * '''Naishōten''': Six women responsible for ritual duties. * '''Shōten Ho''': Eight individuals assisting in rituals. == Post-War Reorganization == With the enforcement of the {{ill|Constitution of Japan|en|Constitution of Japan|qid=Q237082|lt=Constitution of Japan|1=Constitution of Japan}} on May 3, 1947, the Imperial Household Ministry was reorganized into the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}}, and the Shōten-shoku ceased to exist as a state organ.{{Sfn|『神道事典』|p=155|loc=大原康男「宮内庁」|qq=}} Today, these roles are filled by private staff referred to as "Naitei Shokuin" (内廷職員). They are not public officials but direct employees of the Imperial Family, funded through the Imperial Household's internal budget.{{Sfn|『神道事典』|p=159|loc=嶋津宣史「掌典」|qq=}} Current positions: * '''Shōtenchō''': 1 individual. * '''Shōten Jichō''': 1 individual. * '''Shōten''': 6 individuals. * '''Naishōten''': 5 individuals. * '''Shōten Ho''': Additional duties performed by Shikibu Bureau assistants. == Historical Leaders == === Shōtenchō === A detailed list of historical Shōtenchōs, including their appointments, backgrounds, and notable contributions, is maintained in Imperial records and publications. Highlights include individuals from prestigious aristocratic families such as the {{ill|Kujō family|en|Kujō family|qid=Q1791350|lt=Kujō|1=Kujō family}} and {{ill|Sanjō family|ja|三条家|fr|خاندان سانجو|zh|三條家|ko|산조가|de|Sanjō_(Familie)|pt|Família Sanjō|lt=Sanjō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanjō family|qq=}}, as well as recent appointees from bureaucratic backgrounds. === Shōten Jichō === Deputy leaders often advanced to Shōtenchō or transitioned to other prominent roles. Notable figures include aristocrats from families like the {{ill|Tokudaiji family|en|Tokudaiji family|qid=Q11489589|lt=Tokudaiji|1=Tokudaiji family}} and {{ill|Iwakura|ja|岩倉家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwakura|1=Iwakura family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11474158}}. == Related Links == * {{ill|Board of Chamberlains|en|Board of Chamberlains|qid=Q11453306|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Board of Chamberlains}} * {{ill|Shikibu Bureau|ja|式部寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu Bureau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|en|Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|qid=Q11453226|lt=Ceremonies of the Japanese Imperial Palace|1=Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace}}
==References==
{{translated page|ja|掌典職|version=104511020|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:掌典職]]
